name: Welsh Rarebit with Ham and Watercress
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat eggs until well blended.
2. Stir the remaining sauce ingredients with the eggs in a double boiler. A variety of beer is recommended to make the recipe less ordinary.
3. Bring the double boiler to a boil, then reduce heat to medium and heat the mixture. Leave uncovered and stir about once every minute until the desired consistency is achieved.
4. Beat the mixture with a rotary beater or electric mixer until very smooth. Keep in the double boiler to maintain temperature.
5. Toast bread slices (2). Home-made bread sliced slightly thicker than commercial bread is preferable.
6. Place 1 to 2 slices of Black Forest Ham over toasted bread on a dinner plate. Set in a warming oven about 10 minutes before serving.
7. Steam watercress and green pepper until heated through. Microwave for about 2 minutes at high heat.
8. Mound the watercress and green pepper in the center of the ham slices on the serving plates. Spoon the rarebit over the vegetables, ham, and toast, allowing it to flow to cover the plate bottom. Garnish with thinly sliced uncooked tomato.
